input: What user action accepts Privacy Policy updates, and how does Keep notify users beforehand?
expected_output: Users accept Privacy Policy updates by continuing to use Keep’s products and services, and specifically, “your click” means you agree to the changes. Keep says it will notify users of important changes via the Keep App or by other means before the changes take effect.
context:
This Privacy Policy informs you of your choices and our practices regarding any information you provide to us.
Your continued use of our products and services constitutes your acceptance to this Privacy Policy and any updates.
It is a big responsibility to try our best to protect your information and put you in control.
We may from time to time revise or add specific instructions, policies and terms to this Privacy Policy.
Whenever we make any changes to this Privacy Policy that are important for you to know about, we will notify you via Keep App or other means before the changes become effective.
Your click means that you agree with the changes to this Privacy Policy and would like to continue to use our products and services.
  If you have any question about this policy, please send email to:support@gotokeep.com.
We collect the information to provide you a better service  for example, to:
Provide our services.
We need your age, height and weight to calculate your consumption of calorie when you complete each training class.
Maintain and improve our services.
We use your information to ensure our services are working as intended, such as troubleshooting issues reported from you.
Develop new services.
We value your every uploaded training information as it is crucial for us to better understand which training class is more helpful to you.
With adequate data support, we will create and update more popular training classes for you to take.
Personalized service.
We use the information we collect to customize our services for you.
For example, we may provide you with advanced training classes with your updated weight and body measurement in aims to help you achieve your training goals smoothly.
However, we dont show you personalized ads based on sensitive categories, such as race, religion, sexual orientation, or health.
We collect information from you when you use Keep App  for example, when you:
Register to use Keep App;
Log into Keep App with third party platform accounts;
Use Keep App; and
Communicate with us.
The following sets out how we collect such information from you as you use Keep App.
The remainder of this Privacy Policy sets out further information regarding some of these categories.
Registration and Account Information.
You must register for a Keep account in order to use Keep.
To register, you may need to provide certain Personal Information, such as you name, mobile phone number, password, exercise goal and status.
Social Connect.
When you choose to connect your third party social media account to your Keep account, we may collect certain information from your account with that social media platform  for example, to facilitate your login to Keep App with that account.
Using Keep App.We collect information from and about you through your use of Keep App.
For example, we may collect Personal Information with Keep via the following:
Information you make available to us when you open a Keep account, as set out above;
Any information included in your publicly visible Keep profile, which may include your profile ID, name and photo;
Information you make available to us via your use of Keep  for example, via: a) Your sharing of your contact list with Keep in the Recommended Friends function in order to connect with other Keep users; b) Making purchases within Keep, including your credit card or bank details and information about the purchases that you make;
Information we collect in the course of you using Keep  such as certain location data and log data; and
Information you provide through your communications with our customer support team.
Purchases via Keep.
When you make purchases through Keep, you may need to provide certain Personal Information to complete the purchase, such as your credit card information, bank account information.
Where the payment is processed by a third-party payment service provider, we do not collect or store such Personal Information, though we may receive summary information about transactions.
Where we or our affiliate companies process your payments as a payment service provider, or where we fulfill any purchases you have made on Keep, we may collect, use and store your Personal Information in order to fulfill such purchases.
Location Data, Log Data and your device.Keep automatically collects Log Data from your device to enable and assist Keeps functionalities.
In addition, you may also permit Keep to access other content and data on your device  for example, your contact list and photo storage.
Other than consented by you, we commit that we will not sell your personal information to any other third parties for their processing for any purposes.
We will ask for your explicit consent to share any sensitive personal information such as race, religion, sexual orientation, or health.
We will not disclose or transfer your personal information to any non-affiliated third parties, unless:
You may choose to share certain information.
In order to participate in certain features, you may have to adjust your Privacy settings and share more information.
You may also choose to share your activity on other platforms, such as Facebook, Twitter or Instagram

.
Please read the Privacy Policies of those platforms, as your activity published on those platforms will no longer be governed by this Privacy Policy.
We will share personal information if we have a good-faith belief that access, use, preservation, or disclosure of the information is reasonably necessary to a) meet any applicable law, regulation, legal process, or enforceable governmental request, or b) detect, prevent, or otherwise address fraud, security, or technical issues, or c) protect against harm to the rights, property or safety of Keep, our users, or the public as required or permitted by law.
To provide you with a better experience through improving our products, services, content, and advertising or otherwise where you have consented, we may share the personal information with our affiliate companies and with joint venture partners and third-party service providers, contractors and agents, and use it consistent with this Privacy Policy.
For example, we may send and receive data to and from Apple HealthKit to collaborate training consumption calculation on your iOS system with your authorization.
During the register process, we read data from Apple HealthKit to simplify the age and weight input process, in the meantime, your training data will be sync back to Apple HealthKit.
We will never share with or sell the information gained through the use of Apple HealthKit, such as age, weight and heart rate data, to advertisers or other agencies without your authorization.
We will also never use the information for marketing, advertising or similar services.
If Keep is involved in a merger, acquisition, or sales of assets, we will continue to ensure the confidentiality of your personal information and give affected users notice personal information is transferred or become subject to a different Privacy Policy in advance.
When you join or use certain services, you agree to publicly share a basic amount of information, which may include your username, city location, and profile picture.
When using our products and services, you can always manage, review and update information in your account, for instance, you can manage your personal information at any time, such as your Keep ID and photo.
In the meanwhile, you are responsible for the truthfulness, legality, validity and completeness of the information you provide, and update and maintain your personal information in a timely manner to ensure the truthfulness, validity and validity of the information.
  You can export a copy of content in your Keep Account if you want to back it up or use it with a service outside of Keep by sending your request to support@gotokeep.com.
We will help you export the information based on applicable law and your specific request.
You can also remove or delete content from your account or entirely delete your whole Keep account.
If any content you cannot remove or delete by yourself, please kindly reach us via support@gotokeep.com .
In some cases, we retain data for limited periods when it needs to be kept for legitimate business or legal purposes or to be protected from accidental or malicious deletion, there may be delays between when you delete something and when copies are deleted from our active and backup systems.
We use a variety of security measures, including encryption and authentication tools, to help protect your information.
We use secure servers when you place orders.
All credit card information you supply is transmitted via Secure Socket Layer (SSL) technology and then encrypted within our databases.
However, like other companies, we cannot guarantee 100% the security or confidentiality of the information you provide to us.
We maintain server around the world and your information may be processed on servers located outside of the country where you live.
Regardless of where your information is processed, we apply the same protections described in this policy.
We will also keep this Privacy Policy in compliance with significant legal frameworks relating to the transfer of data.
If we receive your complaints about data transfers, we will cooperate with appropriate regulatory authorities when we cannot directly resolve it.
We do not knowingly collect or solicit personal information from children under 13.
If you are a minor, you can use the service only in conjunction with your parents or guardians.
If we are aware that a child under 13 has provided us with personal identifiable Information, we will delete such information from our files.
We use your email address and, in rare situations your mailing address or phone number, to contact you regarding administrative notices from time to time.
To better enrich your experience, there may be products or services provided by third parties.
You can choose whether to access such content or links, or whether to use the third partys products or services.
However, we do not have control over the products or services provided by third parties.
We cannot control any of your personal information held by third parties.
The information protection issues that you use in the use of third-party products or services do not apply to the management of this policy.
This policy does not apply to any information you choose to provide to third parties.
This Privacy Policy applies to all of the services provided by

Keep Inc and its affiliates.
This Privacy Policy does not apply to products and services that have separate Privacy Policies that does not incorporate it.
For example, this Privacy Policy does not apply for a) the information of other companies and organizations that advertise our services, or b) services offered by other companies or organizations with our services to be displayed or linked.

input: List Keep PI collected for acct registration, social login, profile, in-app use, purchases, location/log/device.
expected_output:
Keep may collect the following personal information by category:

- **Account registration:** name, mobile phone number, password, exercise goal, and exercise status.
- **Social login:** certain information from your third-party social media account to facilitate login.
- **Profile:** publicly visible profile information such as profile ID, name, and photo.
- **In-app use:** information you provide while using Keep, such as shared contact list for Recommended Friends, communications with customer support, and other information made available through your account use.
- **Purchases:** credit card information, bank account information, and purchase details; if a third-party payment provider processes payment, Keep may only receive summary transaction information.
- **Location/log/device:** certain location data, log data, and, with your permission, access to device content such as contact list and photo storage.

input: How can public posts, linked social nets, and 3rd-party/ad sites access, share, use PI?
expected_output:
Public posts in Groupon’s social/community areas can be seen by anyone on the Internet, and any personal information you include may be read, collected, and used by others, so Groupon recommends not posting personal information there.

If you link a social network or log in with social credentials, you give Groupon permission to access the profile information you’ve made available for sharing and use it under the social network’s terms and Groupon’s Privacy Statement. Linked accounts may also automatically post your Groupon activity back to your social network.

Third-party websites, business partners, and ad servers may have their own privacy policies and may collect information through cookies, clear gifs, and similar technologies about your visits, device, browser, and activity to deliver targeted ads and analyze usage. Groupon says its Privacy Statement does not cover those third parties’ collection practices, so you should review their privacy policies.

We will retain and use yourPersonal Informationas necessary to comply with our legal obligations, resolve disputes, and enforce our agreements.
Social Community Areas

Prior to posting in these areas, please read ourTerms of Usecarefully.

Social Networks

You also have the option to link social networks, such as Facebook, to your Groupon account.
Once you register with Groupon and connect with the social network, you will be able to automatically post recent Groupon activity back to your social network.
By proceeding through any of the above steps, you grant Groupon permission to access all of the elements of your social network profile information that you have made available to be shared and to use it in accordance with the social networks terms of use and this Privacy Statement.

in connection with a corporate merger, consolidation, or restructuring, the sale of substantially all of our stock and/or assets, or other corporate change, including, without limitation, during the course of any due diligence process.

If you have questions about the security and privacy settings of your mobile device, please refer to instructions from your mobile service provider or the manufacturer of your device to learn how to adjust your settings.

Your device, as well as the Groupon mobile app, provides you with options to control how and when we collect your geolocation.
You may be able to disallow our use of certain location data through your device or browser settings, for example, by disabling Location services for the Groupon application in iOS privacy settings.

If you have any questions about security on our Web site, you can contact us at privacy@groupon.com.

Keeping yourPersonal Informationcurrent helps ensure that we, ourAffiliatesandBusiness Partnersoffer you the offers that are most relevant to you.
If you want to deactivate your Groupon account or have other questions or requests, please contact us.
While we are ready to assist you in managing your subscriptions, deactivating your account, and removing your active profile, we cannot always delete records of past interactions and transactions.
For example, we are required to retain records relating to previous purchases on the Site for financial reporting and compliance reasons.
We will retain yourPersonal Informationfor as long as your account is active or as needed to provide you services and to maintain a record of your transactions for financial reporting purposes.
If you wish to deactivate your account or request that we no longer use yourPersonal Informationto provide you services contact us atsupport@groupon.com.

input: Differentiate 23andMe data types, incl. examples, identifiability, collection methods, and ad/sharing limits.
expected_output:
Here’s a concise breakdown of 23andMe’s data types:

- **Personal Information**: Data that can identify you alone or with other data.  
  - **Examples**: name, email, address, payment info, date of birth, phone number.  
  - **Collected by**: account registration, purchases, customer service, third-party/social features.

- **Registration Information**: Personal details provided when creating an account or buying services.  
  - **Examples**: name, email, address, user ID/password, billing/shipping info, payment details.

- **Genetic Information**: Your genotype data from saliva processing.  
  - **Examples**: DNA markers (“As, Ts, Cs, Gs”), genetic test results/reports.  
  - **Collected by**: saliva sample submitted to 23andMe’s lab.  
  - **Sensitivity**: explicitly treated as **Sensitive Information**.  
  - **Ad limit**: 23andMe and its providers **do not use Genetic Information for targeted advertising**.

- **Self-Reported Information**: Information you directly enter.  
  - **Examples**: traits (eye color, height), ethnicity, disease conditions, cholesterol, family history.  
  - **Collected by**: surveys, forms, features, applications.  
  - **Sensitivity**: some of this is **Sensitive Information** (e.g., health, racial/ethnic origin, sexual orientation, political affiliation).  
  - **Ad limit**: **not used for targeted advertising** when sensitive.

- **Sensitive Information**: Health, Genetic Information, and certain self-reported categories.  
  - **Examples**: health data, genetic data, racial/ethnic origin, sexual orientation, political affiliation.  
  - **Sharing limit**: sharing sensitive data requires a **positive action/opt-in**.

- **Individual-level Information**: Data about one person’s genotypes, diseases, or traits, but not necessarily tied to registration details.  
  - **Identifiability**: about a single person, but not inherently linked to direct identifiers.

- **De-identified Information**: Data stripped of registration and other identifying details so you cannot reasonably be identified.  
  - **Also called**: pseudonymized information.  
  - **Identifiability**: reduced/non-reasonably identifiable.

- **Aggregate Information**: Combined data analyzed as a group.  
  - **Identifiability**: no specific individual can reasonably be identified.

- **User Content**: Content users create/post, excluding Genetic and Self-Reported Information.  
  - **Examples**: forum posts, messages, photos, videos, text.  
  - **Collection/sharing risk**: public blogs/forums may be read and used by others; removal may not be fully possible.

- **Web-Behavior Information**: Data about how you use the services.  
  - **Examples**: browser type, domains, page views, interactions via cookies/beacons/tags.  
  - **Collected by**: cookies and similar tracking technologies, including Google Analytics.  
  - **Ad use**: may support targeted advertising, but **Sensitive Information is not used** for that.

- **Referral / Gift / Social Media Data**: Limited third-party/contact data used for referrals, gifts, or social integrations.  
  - **Examples**: referred person’s email, profile picture, username, friends/followers depending on settings.  
  - **Limit**: referral email is used **solely** for the referral/sharing communication.

input: Summarize Groupon user ctrls for emails, cookies/device data, precise geolocation, plus key opt-out limits.
expected_output:
Groupon says you can control several privacy settings:

- **Commercial emails:** You can opt out by using the unsubscribe link in Groupon emails or by changing email preferences in your account. **Limit:** Groupon may still send **non-commercial emails** about your account or transactions.
- **Cookies / ad targeting:** You can manage cookies through your browser’s privacy/security settings. You can also opt out of certain targeted ads through the **NAI Opt-Out Tool**, **DAA About Ads**, or Groupon’s **AdChoices** page. **Limit:** Groupon says it **does not respond to Do Not Track signals**.
- **Device data:** You can control some device-data sharing and mobile browser cookie settings through your mobile device/browser settings.
- **Precise geolocation:** Groupon may ask permission to collect precise location. You can deny it, allow it only while using the app, or turn it off later in device settings. **Limit:** disabling location may reduce certain site/app features.

Other key limits:
- To stop offers sent **directly by Business Partners**, you must use the opt-out instructions in **their** emails.
- Groupon says it may keep records of past transactions even if you deactivate your account, for legal, financial reporting, and compliance reasons.

input: How can users stop Groupon promo emails, manage cookies, disable precise geolocation, and opt out of ads?
expected_output:
Users can limit these through their account, browser/device settings, and ad opt-out tools:

- **Stop Groupon promo emails:** Follow the unsubscribe instructions in any commercial email, or log into your Groupon account and adjust **email preferences**. Groupon may still send **non-commercial** emails about your account or transactions.

  - You can also use the **NAI Opt-Out Tool** or the **DAA About Ads Opt-Out Tool** to opt out of certain targeted ads.

input: Outline Fiverr data flows: reg/profile, usage/cookies, 3rd-party; purposes, retention exceptions, sharing limits.
expected_output:
- **Registration/profile data:** Fiverr collects information you provide directly when registering or completing your profile, such as email, username, location, login details, and possibly address, phone, authentication details, portfolio, Gig info, ratings, feedback, and anything else you choose to publish. Some profile/activity info becomes public once posted on the Site.

- **Usage/cookies data:** Fiverr also collects data automatically when you use the Site, including IP address, geo-location, device/connection info, browser and web-log data, communications through the Site, and cookie data. Cookies are used for security, functionality, personalization, analytics, and improving navigation/user experience.

- **Third-party data:** Fiverr may obtain supplemental information from commercially available sources, data aggregators, public databases, and demographic/navigation data from third parties. Payment information is handled by payment vendors rather than Fiverr directly.

- **Purposes of processing:** To provide and secure services, verify identity, set up accounts, reset passwords, log activity, customize browsing, improve services, prevent fraud/inappropriate activity, maintain records, comply with legal obligations, and send marketing communications where permitted.

- **Retention and exceptions:** Fiverr keeps personal information only as long as needed for the purpose collected, but may retain it longer for operational records, complaints/challenges, legal compliance, fraud prevention, fee collection, dispute resolution, investigations, troubleshooting, and enforcement of terms. Information from deactivated/closed accounts may be kept for a limited period or longer where legally necessary.

- **Sharing limits:** Fiverr says it does **not sell or rent** personal information to third parties for their direct marketing without explicit consent. It shares data only as needed to operate the Site and provide services, with service providers under restrictions, with authorities/law enforcement when legally required or necessary for fraud/security, with payment vendors for transactions, and in business transfers. Registration contact info is not shared except as described in the policy.

input: How do random IDs, encryption, env/DB separation, and least-privilege MFA reduce re-ID/unauth access, incl. DB segmentation?
expected_output:
They reduce risk by layering protections:

- **Random IDs**: data is assigned a randomly generated ID so an individual cannot reasonably be identified directly.
- **Encryption**: 23andMe encrypts Sensitive Information **at rest and in transit**, helping prevent unauthorized reading of the data.
- **Environment separation**: processing, production, and research environments are kept separate and access is restricted, reducing unnecessary exposure across systems.
- **DB segmentation**: Registration Information, Genetic Information, and Self-Reported Information are segmented across logical database systems to further prevent **re-identifiability** by making it harder to link all data back to one person.
- **Least-privilege + MFA/SSO**: access to Personal Information is limited to authorized personnel based on job function and role, with **multi-factor authentication, single sign-on, and strict least-privileged authorization**, which helps prevent unauthorized access even if credentials are compromised.

"Targeted advertising" service providers We permit third party advertising networks and providers to collect Web-Behavior Information regarding the use of our Services to help us to deliver targeted online advertisements ("ads") to you.

For more information about our marketing practices, please review our Cookie Policy.
Aggregate information We may share Aggregate Information, which is information that has been stripped of your name and contact information and combined with information of others so that you cannot reasonably be identified as an individual, with third parties.
This Information is different from "Individual-level" information and is not Personal Information because it does not identify any particular individual or disclose any particular individuals data.
For example, Aggregate Information may include a statement that "30% of our female users share a particular genetic trait," without providing any data or testing results specific to any individual user.
In contrast, Individual-level Genetic Information or Self-Reported Information consists of data about a single individual's genotypes, diseases or other traits/characteristics information and could reveal whether a specific user has a particular genetic trait, or consist of all of the Genetic Information about that user.
23andMe will ask for your consent to share Individual-level Genetic Information or Self-Reported Information with any third party, other than our service providers as necessary for us to provide the Services to you.

input: What do Fiverr/GA cookies do, what data collect, U.S. storage, and opt-out effects?
expected_output:
Fiverr uses cookies to run the site, remember preferences, personalize content, and measure site performance. Google Analytics cookies specifically help Fiverr understand overall user habits, traffic volumes, which pages are visited most, and whether users get errors, so Fiverr can improve the site.

They may collect data such as IP address, browser/device information, pages visited, time spent on the site, how you arrived at the site, areas viewed, and similar usage data. The Google Analytics information, including your IP address, is transmitted to and stored by Google on servers in the United States.

You can opt out by changing your browser cookie settings or installing Google’s browser plug-in, which creates an opt-out cookie to stop further processing. If you block or prevent these cookies, some Fiverr services or site functions may not work properly, and Fiverr cannot guarantee full site performance.

of users of the Site.
This allows us to continuously improve the Site to provide the best information in support of our project aims.

All of the information that these cookies collect is aggregated, to assist us to improve how the Site works.
Some of these cookies are managed by third parties, and you may refer to the third parties' own website privacy notifications for further information.
In particular, we use Google Analytics cookies to obtain an overall view of user habits and volumes, and to help improve the overall experience on the Site.
Google Analytics is a third-party web analysis service provided by Google Inc, which uses performance cookies and targeting cookies.
The information generated by these cookies about your use of the Site (including your IP address) will be transmitted to and stored by Google Inc on servers in the United States.
  Google will use the information collected for the purpose of evaluating your use of our Site on our behalf, compiling reports on website activity and providing other services relating to activity and internet usage to us.
Google will not associate your IP address with any other data held by Google.
You may refuse the use of cookies by selecting the appropriate settings on your browser as described below.
Furthermore you can prevent Googles collection and use of data (cookies and IP address) by downloading and installing the browser plug-in.
This creates an opt-out cookie which prevents the further processing of your data.
For more information about Google Analytics cookies, please see Google's help pages and privacy policy.
If you prevent these cookies, we cannot guarantee how the Site will perform for you.

Fiverr says those providers are not allowed to use your information for their own marketing purposes.

expected_output:
Keep lets you manage, review, and update your account information, and you can:

- Export a copy of content in your Keep account by emailing support@gotokeep.com.
- Remove or delete content from your account, or delete your entire Keep account.
- Contact support@gotokeep.com if there is content you cannot delete yourself.

Keep also notes that even after deletion, some data may be retained for limited periods for legitimate business or legal reasons, and there may be delays before deleted content is removed from active and backup systems.

input: Outline Keep data collection for acct setup, social login, app/public profile, purchases, location/logs, incl. purposes.
expected_output:
Keep collects data in these main areas, to provide, improve, and personalize its services:

- **Account setup/registration:** name, mobile phone number, password, exercise goal/status, and also age, height, and weight.  
  - **Purpose:** create your account, calculate calorie consumption, and personalize training plans.

- **Social login/social connect:** information from your third-party social media account when you link it to Keep.  
  - **Purpose:** facilitate login and account connection.

- **App use and public profile:** profile ID, name, photo, publicly visible profile info, contact list (if shared for Recommended Friends), communications with support, and training/activity information you upload.  
  - **Purpose:** operate app features, connect you with others, troubleshoot issues, improve services, develop new classes, and personalize recommendations.

- **Purchases:** credit card or bank details and purchase information when buying through Keep; if a third-party payment provider handles payment, Keep may only receive transaction summary information.  
  - **Purpose:** process and fulfill purchases.

- **Location, log, and device data:** location data, log data, and potentially device content like contacts or photos if you permit access.  
  - **Purpose:** enable app functionality, support service operation, and improve performance.

It also states Keep may use data to maintain/improve services, develop new services, and provide personalized experiences, but not personalized ads based on sensitive categories.

- **Opt-out / control methods:**
  - **Promotional emails:** use the unsubscribe instructions in Groupon commercial emails or log into your account and adjust email preferences. Non-commercial account/transaction emails may still be sent.
